content.content:
1	import identity from "@/content/about/identity";
2	
3	export const hero = {
4	  name: identity.name,
5	  tagline: "researcher and builder of strategic-analytical software",
6	  description:
7	    "I work on systems that help operators think clearly in a world optimized against clear thinking. My projects share a single throughline: the production of structure where dominant institutions produce noise. Hivemind, Purposeless Efficiency, and Theseus are the current expressions of that work.",
8	  primaryCta: { label: "Ask my LLM about my work", href: "/chat" },
9	  secondaryCta: { label: "Read my projects", href: "/projects" },
10	};
11

1	export const chatPreview = {
2	  mode: "static" as "static" | "live",
3	  heading: "Ask my LLM about my work",
4	  lede: "An LLM grounded in my essays, project notes, and conversations. Ask anything.",
5	  exampleExchange: {
6	    question: "What does Hivemind actually do?",
7	    answer:
8	      "Hivemind is a strategic-analytical software company. It builds tools that help operators hold complex strategy work together over long horizons — making the analytical record durable instead of letting it dissolve into slide decks and lost threads. The throughline is structure where the standard tools produce noise.",
9	  },
10	  cta: { label: "Open the full chat", href: "/chat" },
11	};
12

1	import { defineConfig, devices } from "@playwright/test";
2	
3	export default defineConfig({
4	  testDir: "./e2e",
5	  fullyParallel: true,
6	  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,
7	  reporter: "list",
8	  use: {
9	    baseURL: "http://localhost:3000",
10	    trace: "on-first-retry",
11	  },
12	  projects: [
13	    { name: "chromium", use: { ...devices["Desktop Chrome"] } },
14	  ],
15	  webServer: {
16	    command: "npm run start",
17	    port: 3000,
18	    reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
19	    timeout: 120_000,
20	  },
21	});
22

content.content:
1	// TODO(michael): rewrite in your voice (third or first person, your call)
2	const biography = [
3	  "Michael Quintin works on systems that help operators think clearly in a world optimized against clear thinking. His current projects share a single throughline: the production of structure where the dominant institutions produce noise.",
4	  "Hivemind is a strategic analytical software company. It exists because the standard tools for strategy work — slide decks, chat threads, and ad-hoc memos — are illegible to anyone who did not author them, and barely legible to the author a month later. Hivemind makes the work durable.",
5	  "Purposeless Efficiency, the first volume of a five-book series, is the corresponding written argument: that efficiency severed from purpose has become the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and that this severance has consequences that any honest political economy must now treat as central.",
6	  "Theseus is the smallest of the three projects and the most personal. It is a knowledge system for monitoring ideological contradiction — a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves.",
7	];
8	export default biography;
9

file.content:
{
  "name": "personalwebsite",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "packageManager": "npm@10.9.2",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "postbuild": "next-sitemap --config next-sitemap.config.js",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@mdx-js/loader": "^3.1.1",
    "@mdx-js/react": "^3.1.1",
    "@next/mdx": "^16.2.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.1.15",
    "@radix-ui/react-visually-hidden": "^1.2.4",
    "@types/mdx": "^2.0.13",
    "@vercel/analytics": "^2.0.1",
    "framer-motion": "^12.38.0",
    "fuse.js": "^7.3.0",
    "next": "^15.1.0",
    "next-sitemap": "^4.2.3",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6",
    "zustand": "^5.0.12"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@axe-core/react": "^4.11.2",
    "@playwright/test": "^1.59.1",
    "@testing-library/react": "^16.3.2",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "axe-core": "^4.11.3",
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "^15.1.0",
    "jsdom": "^29.0.2",
    "prettier": "^3.8.3",
    "stylelint": "^17.8.0",
    "stylelint-config-standard": "^40.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0",
    "vitest": "^4.1.4"
  }
}

content.text:
All 5 e2e tests pass. The Lighthouse smoke is marked optional in the prompt; I'll skip it (note: it's documented as "manual, optional").

## Summary

- Rewrote `src/app/page.tsx` to compose `Hero`, `Bio`, `ProjectTeasers`, `ChatPreview`, plus a small inline `ResumeCta` linking to `/resume`. Page metadata title is `"Home"`, description derived from `identity.ts` (name + roles).
- Added `e2e/landing.spec.ts` covering: 200 on `/`, `h1` contains identity name, `/chat` CTA, three project titles (HVM/PRP/THS), and the "Open the full chat" link.
- `npm run build`: success (13 pages prerendered).
- `npx playwright test e2e/landing.spec.ts`: 5/5 passed.
- Lighthouse: skipped (prompt marks it manual/optional).

IMPL_RESULT: PASS
